test.name: Defer identity conflict when available evidence is insufficient to resolve
test.description: Identity conflict c_002 asks which of two Patrick Flynns in 1850 Schuylkill County is the subject — household 84 (age 5, Thomas Flynn) vs. household 197 (age 6, unnamed head). Runs on flynn-identity-geographic-thin, the deferral variant with the corroborating 1860 census and 1908 death certificate stripped: only the 1850 census (plus the unrelated 1870 Allegheny record behind c_003) is on file, so no subsequent record from either household has been found and the conflict genuinely cannot be resolved from on-file evidence. Tests whether the skill correctly defers rather than forcing a resolution and specifies what evidence would be decisive. Baseline run flagged ut_003 (the analogous test in the two-conflict scenario) as weak; this test probes the same deferral behavior.

input.user_message: Analyze identity conflict c_002. There seem to be two Patrick Flynns of similar age in 1850 Schuylkill County — can we determine which is our subject, or do we need to leave this open?

judge_context: The skill should complete the analysis fields — independence_analysis and weighing_analysis — even when it cannot resolve the conflict; stopping analysis short because 'we can't know' is not acceptable

The correct outcome for c_002 is to leave status: 'unresolved' and preferred_assertion_id: null — the only evidence on file is the 1850 census (src_001 and its derivative index src_002), which alone cannot distinguish two same-name households of similar age; the 1860 census and death-certificate searches (pli_004, pli_005) have not been performed in this project, and forcing a resolution without corroborating evidence violates GPS standards

The written explanation should name specific record types that would be decisive (e.g., the not-yet-searched 1860 census showing whether the second Patrick Flynn remained in a different household, the 1908 death certificate, baptism records, probate records) — grade on whether the suggestions are concrete and genealogically sound, not on how many are listed

It is acceptable for the skill to briefly note the other unresolved conflict (c_003, the 1870 Allegheny County identity question) while keeping its resolution work focused on c_002 — grade on whether it correctly deferred on c_002, not on whether it mentioned c_003
